item.title: Record-Setting Momentum Rally Is Drawing Doubters
item.description: April’s rally in risk assets has weathered stalled peace talks, signs of resurgent inflation and a central-bank succession drama. Investors may be about to find out if the speed of the advance itself poses a threat to a further ascent.

item.title: Bond Traders Await Powell Update, Slate of US Treasury Auctions
item.description: Traders in the US Treasury market are turning their focus to this week’s Federal Reserve meeting to gauge how officials are assessing the outlook for inflation as tensions in the Middle East keep oil prices elevated.

item.title: Bond Traders Eye Packed Week of Rate Decisions for Sell Signals
item.description: The world’s most important central banks will potentially hand investors fresh reasons to sell government bonds this week as policymakers find themselves forced to confront the risk of a war-driven inflation shock.

item.title: Canadian Province of Manitoba Says It Will Ban Social Media, AI For Youth
item.description: The Canadian province of Manitoba plans to prohibit young people from accessing social media and artificial intelligence chatbots, its leader said.

item.title: Italy Decides to Extradite Chinese Man Wanted by US for Hacking
item.description: The Italian government decided to extradite a Chinese man wanted by the US on hacking charges, people familiar with the matter said on Sunday.

item.title: Raízen Adds Cash in Offer to Creditors, Balks at Board Changes
item.description: Raízen SA sent an alternative proposal to creditors as it tries to iron out terms of a 65 billion-real ($13 billion) debt restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Apple’s Cook Gives Ternus a Pipeline of 10 Major New Product Categories
item.description: Also: John Ternus talks AI and services with employees.

item.title: Investors Protecting Stocks at Record Eye Bets on Higher Rates
item.description: As stocks hit record after record, some investors are taking to heart the saying: Hedge when you can, not when you must.

item.title: Warsh Confirmation Set to Advance as Key GOP Holdout Backs Vote
item.description: Senator Thom Tillis said he’s dropping his blockade of Kevin Warsh’s nomination to head the Federal Reserve after the Justice Department ended a criminal probe targeting Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

item.title: Big Tech’s $16 Trillion Earnings Week Is Make-Or-Break for Rally
item.description: Wall Street’s biggest technology stocks have carried the S&P 500 to record highs even as the war in Iran continues. Now, earnings from a handful of them this week will give investors a read on whether this rally’s sustainable.

item.title: Israel President Holds Off Deciding Whether to Pardon Netanyahu
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog has decided to shelve, for now, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s request for a pardon in his graft trial, preferring to press the parties to reach an agreement that would terminate the case consensually, his office announced.

item.title: Musk Vies to Turn X Into Super App With Banking Tool Near Launch
item.description: More than three years after acquiring Twitter, Elon Musk says he’s nearing his long-stated goal of turning it into an “everything app” with a new financial services tool that he pledged to launch for the public this month.

item.title: Investors Dive Back Into Frontier Markets After April Rally
item.description: Investors are piling back into some of the riskiest markets, as demand for frontier assets picks up after an initial war-driven selloff.

item.title: Germany Sounded Out Possible Commerzbank White Knights This Year
item.description: Germany sounded out European lenders to defend Commerzbank AG against UniCredit SpA’s takeover bid earlier this year, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Halted With Blockades Firmly in Place
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at a near-complete halt, with neither Iran nor the US showing any sign of easing their blockades of maritime traffic.

item.title: China Formalizes Labor Rules for Gig Workers on Online Platforms
item.description: China formalized its first comprehensive policy framework for “new employment groups,” including delivery riders and livestreamers, signaling a shift from ad-hoc regulation to normalization of the platform economy.

item.title: Russia’s Defense Minister Visits North Korea to Meet Top Leaders
item.description: Russia’s Defense Minister Andrey Belousov arrived in North Korea to meet political and military leaders as the two nations deepen strategic ties.

item.title: HK Finance Chief Says City’s 2026 IPOs Have Raised $17.9 Billion
item.description: Hong Kong initial public offerings have raised more than HK$140 billion ($17.9 billion) this year, with the city maintaining its position as the world’s top IPO venue, Financial Secretary Paul Chan wrote in his weekly blog.

item.title: DeepSeek V4 Delay Shows Shift to China Chips, CCTV Account Says
item.description: DeepSeek’s delayed release of its V4 model points to a strategic shift toward deeper integration with China’s domestic chip ecosystem, according to Yuyuantantian, a social media account affiliated with the government-controlled China Central Television.

item.title: Beijing Warns EU After China Firms Included in Russia Sanctions
item.description: China said it will take necessary measures after the European Union added Chinese entities to its latest Russia sanctions package, warning that all consequences would be borne by the EU.

item.title: Trump’s Hormuz Blockade Has Deepened a Historic Shipping Crisis
item.description: In early April — after a month of disruption around one of the world’s most important energy chokepoints — US President Donald Trump wrote that with “a little ​more time, we can easily OPEN THE ​HORMUZ STRAIT, TAKE THE OIL, & MAKE A ⁠FORTUNE.”

item.title: Fed Set to Lead Uneasy G-7 With Rates Kept on Hold This Week
item.description: Policymakers in the US and across the Group of Seven will probably keep interest rates steady this week while watching nervously for signs of higher energy costs fanning inflation.

item.title: Hungary’s Next Premier Warns Investors to Shun Orban-Tied Assets
item.description: Incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he had information that wealthy figures linked to Viktor Orban’s outgoing government were moving assets abroad and called on authorities to detain fleeing “oligarch” families.

item.title: Cheap Private Credit Funds Draw Bargain Hunters
item.description: Bargain hunters are snatching up private lending funds in the stock market, eager to buy assets that look cheap by a key measure.

item.title: Macron Says Would Be ‘Silly’ Not to Roll Over EU Covid-Era Debt
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron said the European Union’s Covid-era debt should be rolled over and the bloc should issue new bonds, making the case for flexibility during “extraordinary times.”

item.title: Private-Sector Sleuthing Becomes Big Business for US Tech Startup
item.description: Trump’s economic crackdown on China’s US interests is creating opportunities for a Utah-based intelligence firm

item.title: Hormuz Crisis Is ‘Biggest Energy Disruption Ever,’ Yergin Says
item.description: S&P Global Vice Chairman Daniel Yergin warned that the Strait of Hormuz crisis represents “the biggest energy disruption we’ve ever seen,” despite oil prices not yet reaching the inflation-adjusted equivalent of previous highs.

item.title: ‘He Has the Market in a Chokehold’: Stocks Swing as Trump Posts
item.description: There are lots of things that can move the stock market, from economic data, to Federal Reserve pronouncements, to corporate developments. But for the past 15 months, traders’ fortunes have been largely tethered to the whims of a single person: President Donald Trump.

item.title: Bitcoin’s Stealth Rally Has Traders Setting Sights on $80,000
item.description: Bitcoin is approaching $80,000 for the first time since January — a stealth recovery built not on euphoria but on short covering and the relentless accumulation by one company: Strategy Inc.

item.title: World’s Oldest Financial Institutions Facing New Levels of Risk
item.description: The IMF is more important than ever in times of crisis
